programs.name: Big Sky Waiver Program
programs.what_it_does: Montana's Big Sky Waiver is a Medicaid 1915(c) HCBS waiver providing long-term services and supports to help elderly or disabled residents avoid nursing home placement and live independently in their homes, family homes, or community settings like assisted living.[1][2] Benefits include adult day care, personal care, homemaker services, skilled nursing, home modifications, companion services, meal delivery, respite care, and transitional support for nursing home residents.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Montana residents aged 65+ or physically disabled adults under 65 at risk of institutionalization; 2025 income limit $967/month (individual), asset limit $2,000.[1][2]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific Medicaid waiver (not federal entitlement) with capped slots (2,783 max, including reserves for assisted living and nursing home transitions via Money Follows the Person); tailored to Montana's needs for rural and elderly populations.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Individualized service plan with comprehensive HCBS; prevents institutionalization, no fixed dollar value but covers services like skilled nursing and home mods up to eligibility limits.[1][2]

programs.name: TCARE
programs.what_it_does: TCARE is a program through Montana's Area Agencies on Aging offering guided assessments and support to help caregivers of seniors manage stress and prevent burnout.[3][5]
programs.who_its_for: Caregivers of older adults (60+) and adults with disabilities in Montana.[3]
programs.whats_unique: Montana-specific initiative via state Area Agencies on Aging, focused on caregiver wellness in a rural state with unique aging challenges.[3][5]
programs.benefit_value: Guided assessment and support services; no monetary value specified, emphasizes prevention of caregiver burnout.[3]

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (S.H.I.P.)
programs.what_it_does: S.H.I.P. provides free one-on-one counseling to help seniors understand Medicare, Medicaid, private insurance, and long-term care insurance options.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Older and disabled Montanans seeking insurance guidance.[6]
programs.whats_unique: State-delivered through local agencies with in-home counseling by certified technicians; independent, no sales pressure, tailored to Montana's rural access needs.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Free personalized counseling sessions; saves on insurance errors, no direct monetary benefit.[6]

programs.name: Commodity Supplemental Food Program (CSFP)
programs.what_it_does: CSFP delivers monthly food boxes to low-income seniors to supplement their diet and improve nutrition.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income residents aged 60+ in areas like Silver-Bow County, Montana.[5]
programs.whats_unique: Administered locally via Montana aging/disability services in specific counties; state-coordinated beyond federal CSFP standards.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Monthly food boxes; nutritional supplement valued at market cost of groceries (exact $ not specified).[5]
